title: Stint-level ranking objective: one positive lap per pit window instead of plain binary classification
summary: Each pit stop creates a small query where the model mainly needs to rank the final pre-stop lap above earlier laps in the same stint.
rationale: Each pit stop creates a small query where the model mainly needs to rank the final pre-stop lap above earlier laps in the same stint. CatBoost ranking objectives are designed for grouped ordering problems and can reduce the penalty from class imbalance and duplicated near-negative rows.
implementation_hint: Build query groups as contiguous driver-race-stints ending at a pit stop; mark only the lap immediately before the stop as positive, optionally add position weights for laps within 3-5 laps of the stop. Train CatBoost ranker with `YetiRankPairwise` or `LambdaMart`, then blend ranked scores with the current classifier.
expected_effect: Can sharpen separation inside each stint and improve AUC by learning local ordering of pit-window laps instead of global thresholds.
risk: Poor grouping can leak future information or create unstable tiny queries; wet/safety-car stints may behave differently and need separate handling.
